title: palicourea subcuspidata
intro: Palicourea subcuspidata, also known as the 'Palo de Cera' or 'Gutta' plant, is a species of flowering plant in the Rubiaceae family, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits associated with Palicourea subcuspidata include relief from fever, pain, and skin infections, as well as its potential in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, which can be used topically or taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
